title: Best Restaurants in Trat Thailand (2026)
meta_description: The Trat restaurants that still matter: grilled cuttlefish, crab noodles, old-town sweets, seafood noodles, Laem Ngop seafood, and Ban Nam Chiao community food.
intro: Trat food gets much better once you stop using generic island review lists and start with the mainland city itself. The useful shortlist is built around seafood snacks, crab noodles, old-town sweets, one fuller noodle lunch, one mainland-coast seafood stop, and one community-food layer rather than imported beach-club filler.

items.name: Yai Saeng Grilled Cuttlefish
items.description: The clearest seafood-snack stop in Trat because the city still treats grilled cuttlefish as one of its genuine small-eats signatures.
insider_tips: Best used as a later-day snack during the old-center food loop.
items.current_info: TAT still treats Yai Saeng as one of Trat's signature grilled seafood stalls.

items.name: Sukhumvit Crab Noodles
items.description: One of the strongest mainland meals in Trat because crab noodles and mantis-shrimp rice keep the city food layer specific and local.
items.location: Soi Sukhumvit, Wang Krajae, Mueang Trat
insider_tips: Use this as a proper lunch stop before pushing out to Laem Ngop or Ban Nam Chiao.
items.current_info: TAT's current Trat restaurant article still uses Sukhumvit Crab Noodles as one of the city's key seafood-noodle references.

items.name: Ba Na's grilled banana stall
items.description: A stronger old-town sweet stop than a generic dessert cafe because it keeps the food route tied to the streets that still define old Trat.
insider_tips: Use it between the museum side and Rak Khlong Bang Phra rather than as a stand-alone stop.
items.current_info: TAT still treats Ba Na's grilled banana stall as one of the classic small-eats landmarks in old Trat.

items.name: Rim Kheuan Tom Yum Noodles
items.description: The strongest fuller mainland meal on this shortlist when you want spicy seafood noodles and a proper city-side lunch destination.
insider_tips: Better as a deliberate lunch than as a rushed stop after island travel.
items.current_info: TAT still positions Rim Kheuan as one of Trat's stronger noodle-led meals.

items.name: Rimtalay Seafood & Resort
items.description: The clearest seafood stop on the Laem Ngop side when you want the mainland sea-edge to be part of the food planning.
insider_tips: Use this with the easternmost-land stop instead of treating Laem Ngop only as a transfer corridor.
items.current_info: TAT's Trat guide still uses Rimtalay Seafood & Resort to anchor the Laem Ngop coast with an actual meal stop.

items.name: Ban Nam Chiao community food layer
items.description: Best used as a cluster of community-food stops rather than one fixed restaurant, especially for local noodles, snacks, and Tang Mae Krob.
insider_tips: Treat this as part of the village experience, not as a separate formal dinner plan.
items.current_info: MICHELIN's Trat feature still uses Ban Nam Chiao to highlight the village's food and snack culture.

content_sources.title: Don't Miss These 5 Restaurants in Trat
content_sources.creator: Tourism Authority of Thailand
content_sources.description: Used for Yai Saeng Grilled Cuttlefish, Sukhumvit Crab Noodles, Ba Na's grilled banana, Rim Kheuan Tom Yum Noodles, and Rimtalay Seafood & Resort.
